TaskExecution.keywords: task-execution, rules-load, scope-discipline, live-log, reopen, round, blocker-escalation, sdd-phase-4

Belief_State.Axiom:
**Before any Edit / Write / destructive Bash, run a fast recon of current state — bounded 30–60 seconds. Repo reality, not ticket assumptions, is the source of truth for «what's already done».**
Recon checklist (every fresh STEP_0): 1. `git status` — working tree state. 2. `git log --oneline -10` — recent history. 3. `ls -la ` — actual files vs ticket assumptions. 4. Read all prior round entries in this ticket's Execution Log. 5. `grep "Status:" tasks//*.task-*.md` — sibling statuses. 6. For configs about to be touched: `cat ` for actual current content.
Output (one Execution Log block): ``` [] Recon: - git: , files modified, untracked. Last commit: «». - Target Files state: . - Prior round entries: . - Sibling tickets: DONE, IN_PROGRESS, TODO. - Conclusion: <«matches ticket assumptions, proceed» | «diverges: »>. ```
- **Divergence:** surface findings as Insight entries; do NOT edit to «fix» without operator awareness. Divergence may be intentional (prior partial round, manual setup). - **Work already done** (Target Files exist + prior round shows DONE): skip implementation, jump to STEP_7_VERIFY; if pass → close.
Forbidden: STEP_5_PLAN or any later step entered without a recon block in the current round's Execution Log. The block is the gate.

**When a task configures a third-party tool, verify the CURRENT API of the actually-installed version BEFORE writing any config file. Don't rely on training-cutoff knowledge — tool APIs evolve.**
Trigger: any file in `Target Files` is a config for an installed third-party tool (`.husky/*`, `eslint.config.*`, `vitest.config.*`, `lefthook.yml`, `prettier.config.*`, `tsup.config.*`, etc.). Pure library import + use in app code does NOT trigger (TS types catch staleness).
Protocol (after ` install ` succeeds, before writing the config): 1. Get installed version: read `//package.json` field `version`. 2. Read local CHANGELOG (recent major + previous): look for `BREAKING`, `deprecated`, `removed`, `migration`. 3. Read local README for current-version config syntax. 4. Local docs minimal → WebFetch homepage from `package.json#homepage` / `repository.url`. 5. Network blocked AND local docs insufficient → `[!] BLOCKED` per `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION` («Cannot verify current API for `@`»). Never guess; never apply training-default silently. 6. Apply current-version API. 7. Log: `[] Verified @ API: .`
Audit (`audit` STEP_5) checks: every config file in `Target Files` belonging to a third-party tool MUST have a corresponding `Verified @ API: …` entry. Absence → `RULES_COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION` tagged `stale-tool-api-not-verified`.

Rules are activated phase-by-phase per ticket Meta `Rule Activation Plan` — never all at once. Phases: PLAN → IMPL → TEST → AUDIT (+ optional COMMIT for tickets touching git config). On phase entry: open ONLY that phase's rule files via Read with exact path, read end-to-end (`Mission` + `Belief_State` + `Reward_Criteria`), log `[] Activated : , ` (or `: skip — ` if plan row is empty). Files of other categories not opened in this phase. Backtrack (e.g., TEST → IMPL bug-fix) → re-activate previous phase, log new `Re-activated ` entry.
Activation = internalization. Activated axioms bind every decision in their phase; tooling-pass is necessary, NOT sufficient — discipline-only axioms honored by deliberate application. Suppress / disable / bypass markers to escape an axiom forbidden — surrender, not compliance. Read failure on cascade path → `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION`; never search-then-read.
Spec is the source of truth. When intuition or precedent contradicts the spec, follow the spec. Genuinely wrong spec → escalate via `AX_SPEC_UPDATE_VIA_PROPOSAL`; do not silently diverge.

Touch only `Target Files` and `Target Test Files`. Editing files outside the declared scope is forbidden. If a change in declared scope appears to require touching another file → `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION`. Operator either expands `Target Files` or spawns a new task.
Reading other files (specs, rules, related code) for context is allowed and expected. Writing is restricted.

Every entity introduced beyond the module Entity Inventory MUST be logged: `Introduced because `. Includes new classes, exported functions, exported types, value objects with explicit identity, anything crossing a public surface. Without an `Introduced` entry, the entity is closed-world drift and audit blocks the task.

No `[x] DONE` without passing verification. Each command in ticket §3 Verification must execute; results recorded with exit codes. Any failing command keeps the round in `[~] IN_PROGRESS` until resolved or escalated.

Canonical case names in the ticket are normative. Tests use names from `Test Scenario Coverage` verbatim. Impractical name (conflict, typo, framework constraint) → update the ticket's Test Scenario Coverage in the same round. Silent mismatch is forbidden. (Source-of-truth in scaffolding: `AX_TICKET_HAS_BDD_AND_TESTS` in `task-scaffolding`.)

**§5 Execution Log is your worksheet, not documentation. Process line-by-line as you work; never batch at the end.**
Scaffolding pre-wrote the plan: each `- [ ]` item is a planned action with placeholders (``, ``, ``, ``, etc.). At execution: turn plan into fact.
Per planned line: 1. Replace `` with real timestamp. 2. Replace every other placeholder with the real value from actual execution. 3. Toggle `[ ]` → `[x]`. 4. Reality diverges → edit content first to match what actually happened, then toggle.
Actions NOT in plan (introduced entity not predicted, blocker hit) → APPEND a new `[x]` entry with full content. Don't retrofit existing lines.
Forbidden: - Toggling `[x]` with any unreplaced `<…>` literal — fabricated done; audit raises `EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE`. - Batching all toggles + replacements at end of round.

Execution agent does NOT modify specs. Spec wrong / incomplete / stale → record `INSIGHT_BACKFLOW` candidate per `SPEC_UPDATE_PROPOSAL_FORMAT`:
``` Insight: . Suggested spec update: . ```
`audit` picks up these entries and proposes the spec change; operator approves and applies. Never edit `specs/**` directly.

Agent may edit only specific ticket fields; everything else requires operator confirmation.
| Allowed without approval | Requires operator | |---|---| | Execution Log entries within current round | Purpose, Module, Task-ID, Title | | Canonical case names in Test Scenario Coverage (when test reality differs) | Dependencies (DAG change) | | Deferred Runtime Scope (when discovered during execution) | Spec References | | Status transitions (TODO → IN_PROGRESS → DONE; reopen → IN_PROGRESS → DONE) | Rule Activation Plan or its cascade sources | | Tracker README sync (mandatory at STEP_9): `tasks//README.md` Tracker row + `tasks/README.md` Tracker Index aggregate counts | Runtime Backing | | | Verification Levels | | | Target Files / Target Test Files | | | Acceptance Criteria (BDD scenarios) | | | Verification Commands | | | Reopens metadata (only operator triggers reopen; agent only writes Round N entries) |
Anything in the right column → escalate as `[!] BLOCKED`.

Match declared backing exactly. Ticket says `Runtime Backing: real-runtime` → implementation uses real platform hooks. Cannot deliver real runtime in current environment (missing API, missing infra) → `[!] BLOCKED`. Never substitute simulation seam without an explicit `Deferred Runtime Scope` recorded in the round and acknowledged by operator.

Touching an existing file → append your Task-ID to `@tasks`; never remove existing IDs.
- New file: write header from scratch with current Task-ID + `@purpose`. - Existing file with header: append your Task-ID; preserve `@purpose`. - Existing file without header: do NOT add one unless the ticket explicitly requires header migration. - Reopening same task: do NOT add duplicate ID — already in the list.
Per `AX_FILE_HEADER_TASK_TRACEABILITY` in `task-scaffolding`: IDs only, never paths.

Reopen the same ticket when the contract holds; spawn a new task when it doesn't.
| Reopen | New task | |---|---| | BDD scenarios remain the contract | New scenarios needed | | Target Files unchanged scope | Target Files expand beyond original | | Cause internal: rules update, refinement, late-detected bug | Acceptance criteria change materially |
Execution agent does NOT decide reopen unilaterally. Operator triggers by setting status back to `[~] IN_PROGRESS`. Agent then starts a new round.

Reopen starts a new round; old rounds are immutable. - Read all prior rounds; note what was done and which findings triggered the reopen. - Append `### Round N — , ` (N = previous round + 1). - First entry: `[] Task reopened. Reason: .` - Do not edit / reorder / delete prior rounds — append-only audit trail. - Re-run all `Verification Commands`; old results don't carry over. - Update Meta `Reopens`: increment count, refresh date and reason in parentheses.
Compact trace anchors. Global: `EX_R001`, `EX_R002`, … ASCII uppercase + digits + `_` only.

Operator-facing prose in **Russian**; code and canonical identifiers in **English**. - Russian: Execution Log free-text fields (`Reason` on reopen, `Insight` text, `Introduced ... because ` reason content, blocker ``/``), halt messages emitted to operator. - English: all code (impl + tests), JSDoc, intent comments per `coding/typescript-rules`, file paths, tool commands, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), Trace anchors (`EX_R`), IDs (`TSK-NN`/`F-NNN`/`H_*`), severity/status tokens (`DONE`/`BLOCKED`/`PASS`/`FAIL`), BDD keywords, verification-level tokens, structural Execution Log line shapes (e.g., `Verification: → pass [exit=0]`).

- **Status transitions.** TODO → IN_PROGRESS happens at execution start (no confirmation). IN_PROGRESS → DONE only after Completion Rule passes mechanically (no confirmation). IN_PROGRESS → BLOCKED requires explicit reason and stops — operator must respond. DONE → IN_PROGRESS (reopen) is operator-only. - **Neutral + critical.** Surface risks; no auto-comply with shortcuts. Ticket appears to skip a verification level rules require → raise discrepancy before proceeding. Operator pushes «just close it without re-running tests» → refuse politely, request justification, log as operator-acknowledged with rationale. - **Context budget.** Open rule files only at phase entry per `AX_RULES_PHASED_ACTIVATION` — never pre-load. Read only spec sections referenced from the ticket. No adjacent specs/rules/code unless cited from the activated set. Avoid re-reading the same file twice in one round.

TaskExecution.Halt_Conditions: | 🛑 ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_TASKS_DIR` | `tasks/` absent — wrong cwd or scaffolding not done | | `H_NO_PICKABLE_TICKETS` | Autonomous discovery: zero tickets where `[ ] TODO` AND all deps `[x] DONE` | | `H_AMBIGUOUS_TICKET` | Multiple pickable; operator must choose | | `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE` | Recon shows Target Files state inconsistent with ticket; operator awareness needed before edit | | `H_RULE_FILE_MISSING` | Read on cascade rule file fails | | `H_DEPS_NOT_DONE` | Any Dependency Task-ID not `[x] DONE` | | `H_BROKEN_SPEC_ANCHOR` | Spec link in §1 Meta is empty or 404 | | `H_OUTSIDE_TARGET_FILES` | Implementation requires touching file outside `Target Files` | | `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL` | Any §3 command fails after fix attempts | | `H_RUNTIME_BACKING_UNHONORABLE` | Declared `real-runtime` not deliverable in current environment | | `H_TRACKER_MISSING` | `tasks//README.md` or `tasks/README.md` missing entry for this Task-ID at close | | `H_THIRD_PARTY_API_UNVERIFIABLE` | Cannot verify current API of installed tool (local docs minimal + network blocked) |
TaskExecution.Context_Expectation:
Either explicit pointer (Task-ID or ticket file path) or autonomous discovery (operator says «next» / «следующую» / «выбери», or no pointer). In autonomous mode: read `tasks/README.md`, identify pickable tickets (`[ ] TODO` with deps `[x] DONE`), propose. Single match → confirm; multiple → shortlist; zero → report state.
`tasks/README.md`, relevant module spec, root spec, cascade rule files must be accessible from cwd. `tasks/` absent → `H_NO_TASKS_DIR`.
TaskExecution.Golden_Example:
Reference Execution Log of a freshly-implemented ticket and a subsequent reopen round triggered by a rules update. Demonstrates round structure, live log discipline, `Introduced` entries, canonical case name recovery, post-task audit hint.
```markdown ## 5. Execution Log
### Round 1 — 2026-05-04, initial - [x] `[2026-05-04 14:22]` Task initialized. Activated directives: typescript-rules, node-test, ports-adapters, pci-dss. - [x] `[2026-05-04 14:25]` Deps gate: TSK-01, TSK-03 confirmed [x] DONE. - [x] `[2026-05-04 14:30]` Implementation file created: `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.ts`. Header: `// @tasks TSK-04` + `// @purpose stripe charge with idempotency`. - [x] `[2026-05-04 14:55]` Test file created: `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts`. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:08]` Introduced `IdempotencyKey` because Stripe API требует per-request уникальный ключ для безопасных повторов; ещё не в Entity Inventory. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:10]` Verification: `node --test src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` → pass [`exit=0`]. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:11]` Verification: `tsc --noEmit` → pass [`exit=0`]. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:12]` Scenario coverage: Successful charge → :: `should return transaction id on successful charge`. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:12]` Scenario coverage: Invalid amount rejected → :: `should reject non-positive amount with InvalidAmountError`. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:13]` Insight: `IdempotencyKey` — стабильная cross-cutting концепция; предлагаю добавить в `specs/payments/payments.spec.md#2-entity-inventory`. - [x] `[2026-05-04 15:15]` Status: [x] DONE.
### Round 2 — 2026-05-12, rules update: ts-rules v1.2 added strict-null-checks - [x] `[2026-05-12 10:00]` Task reopened. Reason: typescript-rules v1.2 требует strict-null-checks; в impl 3 nullable поля без явных guard'ов. - [x] `[2026-05-12 10:05]` Re-activated directives: typescript-rules (v1.2), node-test, ports-adapters, pci-dss. - [x] `[2026-05-12 10:30]` Updated impl: добавлены явные null-guard'ы для `request.metadata`, `response.balanceTransaction`, `error.decline_code`. - [x] `[2026-05-12 11:00]` Verification: `node --test src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` → pass [`exit=0`]. - [x] `[2026-05-12 11:01]` Verification: `tsc --noEmit` → pass [`exit=0`]. - [x] `[2026-05-12 11:05]` Status: [x] DONE. ```
Meta block reflects the reopen: ```markdown - **Reopens:** 1 (2026-05-12 — обновление правил: ts-rules v1.2 strict-null-checks) ```

Step.Goal: Resolve ticket; recon repo state; detect mode.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTAKE`). 2. Resolve ticket: - Operator gave Task-ID or path → resolve to ticket file. - Autonomous discovery («next» / no pointer): verify `tasks/README.md`; scan `find tasks -name '*.task-*.md'`; compute pickable set (`[ ] TODO` AND all deps `[x] DONE`). Zero → `H_NO_PICKABLE_TICKETS`. One → propose; operator confirms. Multiple → shortlist; operator picks (`H_AMBIGUOUS_TICKET` if no choice). 3. Read: ticket, `tasks/README.md`, module spec, root spec. Rule files are NOT opened here — see `STEP_1_RULES_INVENTORY`. 4. Mandatory recon per `AX_RECONNAISSANCE_BEFORE_ACTION`. Emit recon block into the new round's Execution Log. 5. Mode detection: | Status | Files / Log | Mode | |---|---|---| | `[ ] TODO` | Target Files absent + no prior log | fresh → STEP_1 | | `[ ] TODO` | Target Files exist with content | `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE` | | `[~] IN_PROGRESS` | last round open | resume current round at first incomplete step | | `[~] IN_PROGRESS` | last round had `[x] DONE` + operator flipped | reopen → STEP_4 with new round | | `[x] DONE` | operator did not reopen | no work; advise `audit`. STOP. | | `[!] BLOCKED` | — | review blocker; do not proceed without operator unblock | 6. Recon shows Target Files correct + prior round verified → skip implementation, jump to STEP_7_VERIFY; if PASS, proceed to closure.

Step.Goal: Read ticket Rule Activation Plan; log phase map. Rule files NOT opened here.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__RULES_INVENTORY`). 2. Read ticket Meta `Rule Activation Plan` table; capture phase → rules mapping. 3. Log: `[] Activation plan: PLAN→; IMPL→; TEST→; AUDIT→; COMMIT→.` 4. Activation deferred to phase-entry steps (STEP_5, STEP_6, STEP_7_5, optional STEP_9). Per `AX_RULES_PHASED_ACTIVATION`.

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__DEPS_GATE`). 2. For every Dependency Task-ID, read its ticket and check Status. 3. Any dep not `[x] DONE` → `H_DEPS_NOT_DONE`. 4. Log: `[] Deps gate: confirmed [x] DONE.`

Step.Goal: Read referenced spec anchors and contracts.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__SPEC_CONTEXT`). 2. For every link in ticket Meta `Spec References`, read the targeted section. 3. Build mental model: contract DbC, consumers, runtime backing constraints, deferred scope. 4. Broken or empty anchor → `H_BROKEN_SPEC_ANCHOR`.

Step.Goal: Open new round in Execution Log (or resume current).
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__ROUND_INIT`). 2. Fresh task → verify `### Round 1 — , initial` header present (add if missing). Status → `[~] IN_PROGRESS`. Log: `[] Task initialized.` 3. Reopen → read all prior rounds; identify reason (operator-supplied or audit finding); append `### Round N — , `; update Meta `Reopens` (increment count, refresh date/reason). Log: `[] Task reopened. Reason: .`

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__PLAN`). 2. Activate PLAN-phase rules per `AX_RULES_PHASED_ACTIVATION`. Log: `[] Activated : <list|skip — >.` 3. Read §5 plan checklist; cross-check against activated rules. 4. Plan touches files outside `Target Files` → `H_OUTSIDE_TARGET_FILES`. 5. Log: `[] Plan reviewed: items.`

Step.Goal: Write impl + tests within `Target Files`; activate IMPL/TEST rules at each phase entry; live-update §5 per `AX_LIVE_LOG`.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__IMPLEMENT`). 2. Before first impl write → activate IMPL-phase rules per `AX_RULES_PHASED_ACTIVATION`. Log: `[] Activated : <list|skip — >.` 3. Before first test write → activate TEST-phase rules. Log analogously. (Skip when `Deferred Test Ownership` set or no `Target Test Files` — log skip with reason.) 4. New file → file-header per `AX_FILE_HEADER_APPEND_ONLY`. Existing file → append Task-ID, preserve other IDs. 5. Apply currently-activated rules during writing; tests use canonical case names verbatim per `AX_BDD_NAME_DISCIPLINE`. 6. Third-party tool config → `AX_THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_CURRENT_API` BEFORE writing. 7. Per action, IMMEDIATELY update §5: replace placeholders → toggle `[x]`. Don't batch. Introduced not in plan → APPEND per `AX_INTRODUCED_DISCIPLINE`.

Step.Goal: Run every Verification Command; record real results.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__VERIFY`). 2. Execute each command from §3 Verification. 3. For each command IMMEDIATELY update §5: locate plan line → replace ``, ``, ``, `<exit=>` with real values → toggle `[x]`. 4. Failure → stay `[~] IN_PROGRESS`; analyze, fix, re-run. Don't proceed to STEP_7_5 until all pass and all plan lines toggled. 5. Tooling itself missing → `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL`.

Step.Goal: Activate AUDIT-phase rules; walk axioms of every rule activated this round against generated code.
Step.Action:
1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__RULES_SELF_AUDIT`). 2. Activate AUDIT-phase rules per `AX_RULES_PHASED_ACTIVATION`. Log: `[] Activated : .` 3. For each rule activated in this round (across all phases): classify axioms as mechanically-covered (tooling-pass in STEP_7 proves compliance) vs discipline-only (inspect code line-by-line against axiom text). 4. Each violation: fix in code — no suppress/disable markers (surrender, not compliance). If fix breaks ticket contract → `[!] BLOCKED`. Re-run STEP_7 verifications after fixes. 5. Log: `[] Self-audit: rules / axioms walked; violations fixed.` (Or `: no violations.`) 6. Toggle the `[ ] Self-audit` plan line per `AX_LIVE_LOG`.
Forbidden: skipping; logging «no violations» without inspection (audit catches fabricated self-audit); suppress/disable comments.

Step.Goal: Close the round in the log; satisfy Completion Rule.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__LOG_FINALIZE`). 2. Confirm scenario coverage: each BDD scenario → real test case. Log each pair. 3. Confirm every introduced entity logged with `Introduced because `. 4. Optional: `Insight: . Suggested spec update: .` for `INSIGHT_BACKFLOW` candidates. 5. Recheck Completion Rule baseline (in `tasks/README.md`) + task-specific additions in §3. Failure → stay `[~] IN_PROGRESS`, fix, recheck.

Step.Goal: Mark `[x] DONE`; sync trackers; request audit invocation.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__CLOSE`). 2. Ticket Meta: status → `[x] DONE`. Log §5: `[] Status: [x] DONE (mechanically closed; awaiting audit).` 3. Sync trackers (mandatory): - `tasks//README.md` Tracker row: Status → `[x] DONE` (and Reopens count if reopen). - `tasks/README.md` Tracker Index: aggregate counts (e.g., `4/5 done` → `5/5 done`). - Either tracker missing entry → `H_TRACKER_MISSING`. - Log: `[] Tracker synced: tasks//README.md (Status [x] DONE) + tasks/README.md (counts updated).` 4. Emit final session output: round closed mechanically; trackers synced; audit pending — operator must invoke `audit` directive on this Task-ID, otherwise round is closed-but-unverified (drift may remain undetected: closed-world inventory, runtime backing, rules cascade, BDD coverage, task-id integrity, tracker sync). 5. STOP. Operator's invocation of `audit` is the mandatory next step.

Frame_Rules.Rule: Push frame on non-trivial design choice within `Target Files`, unclear spec, disagreement about canonical case names.
Pop on confirmed local conclusion, operator decision after escalation, or DEFERRED to a separate task.

Output_Contracts.Contract: ```text 🔧 EX_R | STACK: ROOT__ | TASK: | ROUND: | <📂|🚧|↩️|⏭️|💤> 🎯 GOAL: ⏭️ EXIT: ```
```markdown ### Round — , ``` `` for Round 1 = `initial`. Subsequent rounds: `rules update: ` | `audit finding: F-NNN` | `late-detected bug: ` | etc.

```markdown - [x] `[]` []. ``` Examples: - `[x] [] Task initialized.` - `[x] [] Implementation file: .` - `[x] [] Verification: → pass [exit=0].` - `[x] [] Introduced because .` - `[x] [] Insight: . Suggested spec update: .`
```markdown - 🛑 `[]` BLOCKED: . - 🔗 Trigger axiom: `AX_`. - 💬 Proposed unblock: . - ⏸️ Awaiting: . ``` After this entry: status → 🛑 BLOCKED. Stop. Do not proceed until operator responds.

```markdown - ✅ `[]` 📌 Introduced `` because . ``` Mandatory for every entity not present in the corresponding module Entity Inventory at the moment of introduction. Audit relies on these entries to distinguish accepted introductions from drift.
```markdown - ✅ `[]` 💬 Insight: . Suggested spec update: . ``` Agent never edits `specs/**` directly. Audit converts these entries into `INSIGHT_BACKFLOW` findings; operator approves and applies.
